data.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW — criterion T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]

Verdict: PASS — no findings.

Applicability: T8 is an overlay that fires when the plan DEFINES an LLM/agent system (prompts, sub-agents, reviewers, output schemas, enums). This plan (claim-path enforcement) defines NO such system — it is a pure CONSUMER of an attestation produced out-of-band. The What/Scope sections state "no LLM, no network round-trip" on the claim path and "The heavy review runs OUT-OF-BAND." So the overlay's structural-gap probes are largely not-applicable; where enum-like terms ARE referenced, I tool-verified they are fully specified elsewhere.

Affirmations (criteria/checklist items this chunk satisfies):
- No schema/enum referenced whose value vocabulary is never defined. The signature verdict vocabulary the claim path consumes (certified / mismatch / foreign_key / unsigned) is fully enumerated in src/rebar/signing.py:208-294 (verify_record docstring + returns). The review verdicts (PASS / BLOCK / INDETERMINATE) the out-of-band path emits are defined in src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_verdict.schema.json:5-12 and src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:563. "Material-edit invalidation" maps to the existing `mismatch` verdict (signing.py:287-294, "manifest altered"); "stale" maps to the close-gate head-sha freshness binding (tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_signature.py:358, test_close_gate_story_epic.py:92). The plan's INDETERMINATE/fail-open/cap-hit terms are grounded in the established review-plan design.
- A fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step is specified. The plan decisively specifies each degraded-path fallback: signing-infra UNAVAILABLE → fail CLOSED (consistent with the close-gate posture in rebar.toml:5-12) with --force+justification escape; unsupported stack → fail-OPEN; cap-hit → INDETERMINATE; gate OFF (default) → claim unchanged. This is exactly the "unspecified fallback" gap the probe hunts for, and it is closed.
- No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement. No counters/state increments are introduced (the verify+transition is specified as ONE atomic claim reusing rebar's optimistic-concurrency path).
- A processing protocol/decision rule is co-located with the schema that needs it. The decision rule (which verdicts block vs. pass) lives with the attestation it interprets; nothing is referenced-but-relocated.
- No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipatterns; referenced agents/skills/enums exist (tool-verified). `rebar review-plan` (the out-of-band earner) and `signing.py`'s verify surface both exist and are fully specified.

No T8 findings to action.
